===''Pokémon Horizons: The Series''===
[[File:Penny anime.png|thumb|250px|Penny in {{aniseries|HZ}}]]
Penny debuted in ''[[HZ046|Welcome to Naranja Academy!]]'', where she watched [[Liko]] and [[Ann]]'s battle at [[Naranja Academy]] alongside the [[Paldea]] [[Elite Four]].

In ''[[HZ056|Liko vs. Rika! Towards the Battle's End]]'', Penny met [[Dot]] in person after finding {{an|Terapagos}} hanging around Naranja Academy during the events of [[Elite Four Tag Battle]]. She revealed to Dot that she is spying on [[Chalce]] after her data was suspiciously entered into the academy's systems. After seeing Dot interested in the [[Scarlet Book]], Penny exchanged phone numbers with Dot and they both promised to speak again.

In ''[[HZ066|Infiltrating the System! Naranja Academy in Danger!]]'', Penny informed Dot that the academy had been infiltrated by the [[Explorers]], with Chalce trying to steal data from the school database for information regarding Terapagos. While Dot kept Chalce busy by battling her, Penny tried counter-hacking to protect the data, but their efforts were in vain. Chalce successfully obtained the data she needed and escaped without issue. Despite this setback, Penny was still grateful towards for Dot helping her get proof of Chalce's crime, keeping the damage done to the school to a minimum, and ensuring the school battle tournament was not interrupted.

In ''[[HZ067|Shine on, Terastallization! Liko vs. Roy!]]'', Penny was revealed to have told everyone in Naranja Academy about Dot and her friends' heroic actions. She then watched Liko and [[Roy]]'s battle alongside {{an|Nemona}} and [[Arven]]. After the tournament finished, Penny said farewell to Dot, revealing that she is aware of Dot's secret identity as Nidothing and that she is subscribed to Nidothing's channel.
[[File:Dot and Penny.png|left|thumb|250px|Penny and [[Dot]]]]
In ''[[HZ079|Over the Top!]]'', Penny welcomed the [[Rising Volt Tacklers]] along with Paldea's [[Elite Four]] and [[Geeta]], after which, Penny watched Liko, [[Roy]], and Dot's battle against Geeta and when they gained access to [[Area Zero]], Penny opened the doors to the place and wished Dot good luck on her journey there.

Penny was mentioned by Dot in ''[[HZ085|The Pokémon Paradise of Laqua!]]'', as Dot found a group of an {{p|Eevee}} and its [[Eeveelutions|Evolutions]] in [[Laqua]], Dot decided to take a picture of it for her friend.

In ''[[HZ101|Terastallization vs. Mega Evolution!]]'', Penny met up with Dot again when the Rising Volt Tacklers returned to Naranja Academy. With some of the information presented about [[Laquium]] organized by Dot, Penny was able to track down some sightings and map them so that Dot and her friends could more easily find some places affected by the substance. Soon after, Penny watched [[Roy]]'s battle against Nemona along with the others and seeing Dot's excitement about the battle, decided to challenge her to a battle as well.

In ''[[HZ102|The Knight in the Ruins]]'', Penny and Dot's Double Battle commenced, with Penny's {{p|Sylveon}} and {{p|Flareon}} against Dot's Quaxwell and Tinkatuff. Penny held the advantage for most of the battle due to Quaxwell being affected by Sylveon's {{a|Cute Charm}}, with Tinkatuff having to knock some sense into her teammate. After Sylveon Terastallized and exchanged hits with Dot's Terastallized Quaxwell, Flareon was able to support its teammate and defeat Quaxwell with Swift, winning Penny the battle.

In ''[[HZ115|Welcome to Blueberry Academy!]]'', it was revealed that Penny traveled with Nemona to [[Blueberry Academy]], where she was put in charge of maintaining the League's server systems. In the [[HZ116|following episode]], Penny spoke with Dot through the Rotom Phone, explaining her absence. Later, she meets Dot again when they are both hypnotized by [[Dot's Gimmighoul]].
[[File:Penny Dot disguise HZ120.png|250px|thumb|Penny and Dot and their disguises to infiltrate the [[Exceed]] Museum]]
In ''[[HZ117|Ult-imate Training]]'', Penny joined Liko, Dot, and [[Carmine]] at the meeting with Professor [[Briar]]. While Briar joined Liko in researching [[Pagogo]] and Carmine was battling Dot, Penny stood in the corner, amused by [[Liko's Meowscarada]].

In ''[[HZ119|Don't Give Up, Crocalor!]]'', Penny participated alongside the Rising Volt Tacklers in the meeting with Briar and Carmine regarding the Terapagos data she had researched. Some time later, before the group left the Blueberry Academy, Penny watched [[Drayton]]'s battle against Roy. During the farewell, however, Dot received a call from [[Cervantis|Amethio's father]] revealing information about the [[Exceed]] Museum, and Dot hinted that Penny was the only one who could help, much to her surprise.

In ''[[HZ120|Dot and Penny's Top-Secret Mission!]]'', Penny and Dot, following Cervantis' instructions, managed to infiltrate the Exceed Museum. During the operation, Penny obtained the data they were looking for, but were discovered by the Explorers. When confronted by Chalce, [[Indi]], and [[Rubina]], Penny was separated from Dot during the battle and stayed behind to face the Explorers duo of Indi and Rubina. With the combined effort of Sylveon and {{p|Jolteon}}, Penny managed to defeat them both without problems, and with the help of Dot's newly evolved {{TP|Dot|Gholdengo}}, they managed to escape the location. A little later, while saying goodbye to Dot, Penny assured her friend that [[Jacq]] would receive the collected data and that Dot could contact her again if she needed help.

In ''[[HZ126|Strong Bonds, Can't Lose!]]'', Penny met with the Rising Volt Tacklers and the other members of the Naranja Academy to discuss the information she and Dot had obtained at the Exceed Museum. After the [[Strong Sphere]]s were banned from the institution, Penny watched Liko's battle against [[Dash]] alongside Nemona and Dot. Later, Penny and Nemona decided to join Liko and her friends to stop the launch of the Strong Sphere. In the [[HZ127|next episode]], Penny battled against Dot using her {{p|Vaporeon}} to battle against her Quaxwell. Penny was able to win the battle by having Vaporeon target Tinkaton and Gholdengo who were watching the battle, resulting in Quaxwell's defeat. Dot questioned Penny's actions, with Penny teaching Dot a lesson on how their enemies the Explorers wouldn't be afraid to fight dirty with underhanded tactics, so she should be prepared to deal with those situations.

In ''[[HZ131|A Surprise Reunion]]'', Penny traveled to [[Levincia]] with Nemona and Ann, and later joined the others to watch Roy and [[Master Mayonnaise]]'s battle. During the battle, Penny was quite impressed with the veteran Trainer's {{p|Hawlucha}}. In [[HZ132|the next episode]], while traveling to [[Galar]] with the Rising Volt Tacklers, Penny decided to explore [[Diana]]'s castle, and later, when she saw [[Ult]]'s training battle against Diana and Master Mayonnaise, she once again showed her admiration for Master Mayonnaise's Hawlucha.

In ''[[HZ133|The Final Battle Is Near]]'', Penny joined in the battle against the Explorers, joining Dot and Nemona in battling against Indi and Rubina's {{p|Cofagrigus}} and {{p|Runerigus}}. The trio struggled against their opponents at first due to their use of {{m|Curse}} and {{m|Destiny Bond}}, but with [[Hamber]]'s arrival, they were able to defeat their Pokémon. In the [[HZ134|next episode]], Penny and her group meet back up with their friends, where she meets [[Amethio]]. Penny and the group then encounter [[Spinel]], Rubina, and Indi.

In ''[[HZ135|The Core Awakens]]'', Penny and her friends start erasing the Laquium Core, but the Explorers got in the way, even defeating her Sylveon in the process. However, Penny and the group became surprised when [[Coral]] turned against Spinel and used her {{p|Scream Tail}}, nicknamed Screamypuff, to take Sylveon's place. Penny and the group became even more surprised when [[Chalce]] and [[Sidian]] also turned against Spinel as they decided to team up with the heroes in order to stop him for good. During the process to remove Laquium, Spinel merged with its Core, casuing it to awaken, much to Penny and the group's shock. Penny and the group tried to fight it, but it was too strong. However, they refused to give up and found out that the middle of the Laquium Core was the weak point that they needed to hit. In the [[HZ136|next episode]], Penny watched her friends destroy the Laquium's Core defenses and purify it for good. After the threat with Laquium was over, Penny returned to Naranja Academy.

====Character====
[[File:Penny party.png|250px|thumb|Penny and some of her Pokémon]]
Penny is a [[Pokémon League]] engineer. In addition to that, she is also responsible for registering [[Tera Training]] students, a work that Penny takes seriously, making sure she knows the names of all the students taking the course.

Penny's personality in general is quite reserved, shy, anxious, and quiet, just like in the games, which made her quickly resonate with [[Dot]] and befriend her easily. Also like her game counterpart, she is incredibly skilled in technology and hacking, something she shares with Dot. Her nervousness can make her somewhat absent minded and forgetful in social interactions, completely forgetting to share her name with Dot during their first meeting. Despite this, she is extremely perceptive and observant, quickly catching onto [[Chalce]]'s disguise and becoming suspicious of her upon noticing how her data was entered into the school systems, as well as easily finding out about Dot's identity as Nidothing despite not being told about it by the former.
[[File:Ann Nemona Penny.png|left|250px|thumb|Penny with Nemona and Ann]]
In ''[[HZ101|Terastallization vs. Mega Evolution!]]'', Penny demonstrated a different trait of her personality when she saw Dot's excitement over a Pokémon battle, displaying a smile quite different from her usual more serious posture, which led her to challenge her friend to a battle as well. In the following episode, she was shown to be surprisingly adept at battling, with Nemona claiming that she is skilled enough to tutor and even rival the top trainers of the Pokemon League, being able effectively utilize her {{p|Sylveon}}'s {{a|Cute Charm}} Ability against {{TP|Dot|Quaxwell}} and using her outstanding observational skills to read her opponent, as well as displaying exceptional teamwork and synergy with her Pokémon, allowing her to defeat Dot despite both of her Pokémon being at a type disadvantage. In ''[[HZ120|Dot and Penny's Top-Secret Mission!]]'', her battle prowess was further displayed, able to hold her own against several of the Exceed Museum's guard Pokémon, as well as singlehandedly defeat Indi and Rubina's Laquium-enhanced Pokémon even without Terastalizing and being at a severe disadvantage.

At her core, Penny is a kind, selfless, and compassionate individual who cares very deeply about her friends, as shown by her willingness to assist Dot and the Rising Volt Tacklers against the Explorers even when it put her in direct danger multiple times. She even went out of her way to spread word of their heroic actions in ''[[Shine on, Terastallization! Liko vs. Roy!]].'' Penny also has a very strong moral code, developing a strong dislike for the Explorers due to their actions against the Rising Volt Tacklers and Pokémon. She is even able to see through the Explorers lies, due to seeing her friends for who they truly are and witnessing their heroism firsthand. This dislike is further exacerbated after the Explorers frame her friends and witnessing the effects of Laquium, causing her and Nemona to join the Rising Volt Tacklers in their opposition against Exceed and the Strong Spheres, showing that her compassion extends to Pokémon as well. Despite her compassion for her friends, she is also not afraid to show tough love to them, employing underhanded tactics to defeat Dot in ''[[HZ127|The Training Royale!]]'' to show her that the Explorers would not hesitate to do the same. In spite of her shyness regarding humans, she has a much easier time opening up around Pokémon, usually preferring to be around them and is easily fascinated and entertained by them.

====Pokémon====
Just like in the games, Penny has often been referenced as a fan of {{p|Eevee}} and its [[Eeveelution|evolved forms]], including having an extensive themed collection of souvenirs involving this species of Pokémon. This is reflected in her Pokémon, where all of Penny's known Pokémon are the evolved forms of Eevee.

This listing is of Penny's known {{OBP|Pokémon|species}} in {{aniseries|HZ}}:

|desc={{p|Sylveon}} is Penny's first known Pokémon and her main partner. Inspired by {{an|Nemona}} and [[Roy]]'s [[Double Battle]], she challenged [[Dot]] to one as well, partnering Sylveon with {{p|Flareon}} in ''[[HZ101|Terastallization vs. Mega Evolution!]]''.

The battle begun in the [[HZ102|next episode]]. After exchanging a couple of attacks, Sylveon's {{a|Cute Charm}} [[Ability]] activated, hindering {{TP|Dot|Quaxwell}}'s performance in battle and allowing for Penny's Pokémon to gang on {{TP|Dot|Tinkatuff}}. In the end, both Sylveon and Quaxwell {{Tera}}stallized, but Flareon landed the finishing blow, defeating Quaxwell and making Penny win by default.

In ''[[HZ120|Dot and Penny's Top-Secret Mission!]]'', Sylveon accompanied Penny during the mission at the [[Exceed]] Museum. When Dot and Penny were cornered by [[Indi]], [[Rubina]], and [[Chalce]], Penny used Sylveon along with {{p|Jolteon}} to battle alongside Dot's Pokémon. When Chalce separated Dot from Penny, Penny battled with Sylveon and Jolteon against Indi and Rubina's Pokémon. Despite Dot's concern, Penny managed to win the battle against the two [[Explorers]].

In ''[[HZ135|The Core Awakens]]'', Sylveon joined the other Pokémon in their mission to erase the [[Laquium]]. When Indi and Rubina attempted to stop them with their {{p|Electrode}}, Sylveon tried to stop them, only to be defeated by their {{m|Explosion}}, temporarily jeopardizing the mission until Coral and her Screamypuff arrived to fill the gap Sylveon's defeat left behind.

Sylveon's known moves are {{m|Moonblast}}, {{m|Disarming Voice}}, and {{m|Reflect}}, and her Ability is {{a|Cute Charm}}.}}

In ''[[HZ133|The Final Battle Is Near]]'', Vaporeon was the second Pokémon Penny used in her battle against [[Indi]] and [[Rubina]]'s {{p|Cofagrigus}} and {{p|Runerigus}}. It became affected by the opponent's Curse immediately, dealing damage to it while being unable to attack or switch out due to Destiny Bond and Mean Look. After Dot's Gholdengo sacrificed itself to defeat the Runerigus and Cofagrigus, Vaporeon was able to be recalled and freed from Curse's effects.

Vaporeon's known moves are {{m|Trailblaze}}, {{m|Whirlpool}}, and {{m|Muddy Water}}, and its Ability is {{a|Water Absorb}}.}}
